implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Static frontend served by Express. It does not initiate any API calls nor trigger the user‑fetch→CSV flow.
implementation.name: index.html
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render static HTML page
No logic related to fetching users or CSV generation
No interaction with backend endpoints

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Exposes the CSV data already stored by fetch-users.ts. Reads existing CSV, converts it to JSON, and sends it to the client.
implementation.name: GET /api/users
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF users.csv does not exist:
    return 404 with error
READ users.csv from disk
PARSE CSV into JSON using csvToJson
RETURN parsed JSON to caller

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Core implementation of the requirement. Fetches users from JSONPlaceholder, converts them to CSV, writes the file to persistent storage.
implementation.name: main()
implementation.pseudo_code:
SEND HTTP GET request to USERS_URL
IF response not ok:
    THROW error
PARSE response JSON into users array
BUILD CSV headers
FOR each user in users:
    convert into CSV row via userToRow
JOIN rows into CSV string
ENSURE data directory exists
WRITE users.csv to disk
LOG success

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Converts a User object into a list of CSV‑safe fields. Ensures each JSON field becomes a CSV column.
implementation.name: userToRow()
implementation.pseudo_code:
FOR each required field:
    escape CSV value
RETURN array of string fields

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Prevents malformed CSV data by quoting values containing commas, quotes, or newlines.
implementation.name: escapeCsvField()
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF value contains comma, quote, or newline:
    wrap in quotes and escape internal quotes
RETURN cleaned value

missing_implementations: before saving information check filter for data with location as Boone
If the API request fails or the response is empty, the system does not create or modify the CSV file and signals an error.
CSV file contains columns corresponding to each field in the user JSON object (e.g., id, name, username, email, etc.).
No user data is omitted or transformed; all fields from the API response are included.
